title: DNS Availability Monitoring
dataSources: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (DNS tests)
spl:
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="dns-server"
| stats avg(dns.lookup.availability) as avg_availability by dns.question.name, server.address
| where avg_availability < 100
| sort avg_availability
description: DNS failures cascade into application outages ‚Äî if users cannot resolve names, nothing works. ThousandEyes DNS tests monitor availability from multiple global vantage points.
value: DNS failures cascade into application outages ‚Äî if users cannot resolve names, nothing works. ThousandEyes DNS tests monitor availability from multiple global vantage points.
grandmaExplanation: Failures cascade into application outages ‚Äî if users cannot resolve names, nothing works ‚Äî so outages, slowdowns, and risky changes get flagged in time to act.
implementation: Create DNS Server tests in ThousandEyes targeting critical domain names and DNS servers. The OTel metric `dns.lookup.availability` reports 100% when resolution succeeds and 0% on error. The Splunk App Network dashboard includes a "DNS Availability (%)" line chart with drilldown to ThousandEyes.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (DNS tests).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Create DNS Server tests in ThousandEyes targeting critical domain names and DNS servers. The OTel metric `dns.lookup.availability` reports 100% when resolution succeeds and 0% on error. The Splunk App Network dashboard includes a "DNS Availability (%)" line chart with drilldown to ThousandEyes.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`stream_index` thousandeyes.test.type="dns-server"
| stats avg(dns.lookup.availability) as avg_availability by dns.question.name, server.address
| where avg_availability < 100
| sort avg_availability
```

Understanding this SPL

**DNS Availability Monitoring** ‚Äî DNS failures cascade into application outages ‚Äî if users cannot resolve names, nothing works. ThousandEyes DNS tests monitor availability from multiple global vantage points.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=thousandeyes`, ThousandEyes OTel Tests Stream ‚Äî Metrics (DNS tests). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco ThousandEyes App for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7719). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Invokes macro `stream_index` ‚Äî in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dns.question.name, server.address** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where avg_availability < 100` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (availability % over time), Single value (current availability), Table (question, server, availability).
visualization: Line chart (availability % over time), Single value (current availability), Table (question, server, availability).
